items.title: CMS issues interim final rule creating an 80-hours-per-month Medicaid community engagement requirement
items.summary: CMS issued an interim final rule on June 1, 2026 requiring certain adults to document 80 hours per month of qualifying activities (work, job programs, community service, or school at least half time) to enroll in or keep Medicaid coverage. The rule allows meeting the requirement via income of at least 80 times the federal minimum wage (listed as $580 per month in 2026) and generally requires states to implement by January 1, 2027.

items.title: NPR: Medicaid work requirements take effect Jan. 1 with an 80-hours-per-month standard
items.summary: NPR reports the new federal Medicaid work requirements take effect January 1 and require adults to show at least 80 hours per month of work, school, or volunteering to stay eligible. NPR also cites a Congressional Budget Office estimate that the policy could leave more than 5 million additional people without health insurance.

items.title: HUD updates guidance on the Asset Repositioning Fee timeline for public housing demolition/disposition
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-16 (issued May 29, 2026) updates guidance for when a public housing agency can receive the Asset Repositioning Fee (ARF) as part of operating subsidy during demolition or disposition. ARF eligibility begins the first day of the next quarter after the trigger date plus 6 months, and can last 24 months for disposition or 36 months for demolition.

items.title: USDA seeks comments on SNAP retailer waiver requests for incentive projects (deadline July 27, 2026)
items.summary: USDA/FNS published a Paperwork Reduction Act notice renewing an information collection for SNAP-authorized retailers to request a waiver to offer incentives that encourage healthier purchases. Written comments must be received on or before July 27, 2026, and the collection affects retailers and partner organizations (not SNAP households directly).

items.title: IRS: 27 states elect to participate in the new Federal Scholarship Tax Credit (up to $1,700)
items.summary: The IRS announced that 27 states have elected to participate in the Federal Scholarship Tax Credit program, allowing eligible taxpayers to claim a federal tax credit of up to $1,700 for qualified contributions to Scholarship Granting Organizations (SGOs). To claim the credit, taxpayers must contribute to an SGO located in a participating state that has submitted a list of qualified SGOs.

items.title: SSA: 2026 Trustees Report projects combined OASI and DI trust fund reserves can pay full benefits until 2034
items.summary: The Social Security Board of Trustees’ 2026 report projects the combined OASI and DI trust fund reserves can pay 100% of scheduled benefits and administrative costs until 2034. After that, dedicated income would be sufficient to pay about 83% of scheduled benefits.

items.title: HHS publishes 2026 federal poverty level standards used for program eligibility
items.summary: HHS has published the 2026 federal poverty level standards, which many programs use to set income eligibility and cost-sharing thresholds. The updated poverty guidelines can affect eligibility cutoffs for programs like Medicaid/CHIP and other assistance programs that reference the federal poverty level.
